items.title: Liberal senator speaks out against Taylor's budget reply

children.content: Senator Andrew McLachlan raises concerns over Angus Taylor's use of the term "mass immigration" and plan to push tax-paying migrants off welfare benefits.

articles.title: Plibersek says government has more explaining to do on budget

children.content: Social Services Minister Tanya Plibersek says the government still has explaining to do over measures announced in last week's federal budget. Follow live.

image.altText: A blonde woman wearing a pale blue suit jacket, speaking.

items.title: Iran weekly briefing: New front emerges as UAE strikes back

children.content: The UAE shifts from target to combatant amid reports the US and Israel are preparing to renew strikes on Iran, writes Matthew Doran.

image.altText: Man with Jerusalem in the background

items.title: Fragile ceasefire holds, but fallout of Iran war is reshaping millions of lives

children.content: It has been more than two months since the US and Israel launched strikes in Iran. But the impact of the conflict continues globally.

image.altText: The silhouette of a man looks into the distance at black smoke billowing into the sky.

items.title: 'Boomer bashing': Are cuts to private health rebates addressing generational or wealth inequality?

children.content: The federal government plans to reduce private health insurance rebates for older Australians, a move one advocate fears could "tip many over the edge".

gallery.altText: An older woman in a grey jumper

thumbnail.altText: An older woman in a grey jumper

items.title: No Walsh for Queensland as Blues coach accidentally lists former Maroon

children.content: James Tedesco wins back his NSW fullback spot in a State of Origin I squad featuring seven potential debutants, with coach Laurie Daley accidentally announcing the name of an ex-Queensland player. Follow live.

image.altText: Casey McGuire and the Queensland Maroons at State of Origin training.

items.title: Why the US, UK and NZ haven't raised interest rates in years

children.content: As Australians pay more on their mortgages, some countries have had no rise in their interest rate in recent years. So what's going on?

image.altText: Three balloons with the UK, NZ and US flags wrapped around them rise against a lined background

items.title: Tax is a great motivator but will Labor's changes fix Australia's housing mess?

children.content: The Albanese government and the opposition both want to turn back the clock on the Australian tax system.

image.altText: Albanese is in focus as he sits on the green bench watching Chalmers speak

avatar.alt: A man in a suit jacket wearing glasses

items.title: Mass showers and squalor: What spurred ex-Labor minister's NDIS fight

children.content: A former NSW Labor powerbroker and state disability minister pivotal in advocating for the establishment of the NDIS has warned against rushing through sweeping changes to the scheme.

image.altText: A middle aged man with short grey hair sitting on a verandah

items.title: The $10b offer Dave Hughes would 'hand on heart' turn down

children.content: Comedian Dave Hughes spoke to The Assembly about why he quit alcohol at 22, being "addicted to work" and the type of dad he's trying to be for his three kids.

gallery.altText: Dave Hughes holds a microphone and is lit with stage lights.

thumbnail.altText: Dave Hughes holds a microphone and is lit with stage lights.

avatar.alt: Woman with neat, short hair smiles warmly for professional head portrait. Blurred trees are in the background

items.title: Push for Australia to extract helium as Iran war impacts global supply

children.content: Helium is needed in health care and to make microchips and semiconductors, but the Middle East war has affected supply. Some think it is an opportunity for Australia's gas industry.

image.altText: A helium well in a grain field

items.title: ASX set to fall after sharp drops on Wall Street, AUD down

children.content: Australian markets are set to begin the week in negative territory, with ASX 200 futures pointing to a drop of around 0.1 per cent on Monday after both US indexes fell sharply from record highs. Follow the day's financial news and insights from our specialist business reporters on our live blog.

image.altText: A woman is seen in the reflection of a window looking at shares' performance on a board of the ASX

items.title: Homes destroyed, living in rubble, Gazans worry about dead awaiting burial

children.content: The families of Palestinians believed to be trapped under the rubble of collapsed buildings want to bury their dead.

gallery.altText: A woman wearing a black hijab stares ahead while sitting among rubble in Gaza.
A close up of rubble and debris in Gaza.

items.title: Man found dead in fire at block of units in Ballarat's Wendouree

children.content: The man was pulled from the house by emergency crews, but could not be revived.

image.altText: brick unit with police officers and police tape

items.title: Melbourne nightclub has operated for two years without insurance

children.content: Pride of our Footscray stopped paying public liability insurance when 18 insurers refused to underwrite the venue. The manager says operating without cover keeps staff on tenterhooks.

image.altText: A drag performer in a large blonde wig, pink glitter eyeshadow, false lashes and pink lipstick in a pink dress.

items.title: How an Australian designer's fashion fame spurred a mental health unravelling

children.content: Rochelle Gregory has clothed everyone from Paris Hilton to Dua Lipa. Now the designer is using film to candidly explore her mental health battle while showcasing her latest collection.

gallery.altText: A woman in a black leather jacket struts down a runway while women clap.

thumbnail.altText: A woman in a black leather jacket struts down a runway while women clap.

items.title: Reasons it's so hard to end a long-term relationship, even when you want to

children.content: It's not just practical and emotional reasons that get in the way of executing a break-up. There are many ways your brain is making you want to stay.

image.altText: woman looking sad with man blurred in the background

items.title: Quiz: It's history time! Where was Pac-Man first released?

children.content: In what year did John Howard become a federal MP? And which country got to play Pac-Man first? Test yourself in this week's history quiz.

image.altText: Neon lights in the shape of Pac-man (a yellow circle with a missing segment for a mouth) and two ghosts, in pink and blue.

items.title: Qld developer donation overhaul yields $170,000 in cash

children.content: More than $170,000 in donations from developers has been given to Queensland state politicians since laws banning developer donations were overturned in March.

gallery.altText: Jarrod Bleijie stands in front of an easel holding plans for a development.

thumbnail.altText: Jarrod Bleijie stands in front of an easel holding plans for a development.

items.title: Meet the women rangers in Central Australia working to protect country

children.content: Dozens of Central Land Council Aboriginal rangers gathered east of Alice Springs last week for the 2026 Women's Ranger Camp — a time for health, learning and healing in the wake of the town's recent loss of Kumanjayi Little Baby.

image.altText: A group of women trekking towards a rocky gorge, lush greenery surrounds them on either side.

items.title: Pilots parachute from jets after mid-flight collision at US air show

children.content: Two fighter jets have collided mid-air during an air show at a US Air Force base, forcing pilots to eject and parachute to safety.

image.altText: Four jets fly in formation in the sky.

items.title: French Epstein probe sees 10 new alleged victims come forward

children.content: About 10 new suspected victims have come forward as part of a French probe into the network of late US sex offender Jeffrey Epstein.

gallery.altText: A middle-aged man with grey-white hair wearing a grey T-shirt looks serious in front of a plain background.

thumbnail.altText: A middle-aged man with grey-white hair wearing a grey T-shirt looks serious in front of a plain background.

items.title: Ukraine launches massive drone wave against Russia, killing four

children.content: Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said the attacks were justified as "responses to Russia's prolongation of the war and its attacks on our cities and communities", two days after Russian strikes on Kyiv killed 24.

image.altText: A house burns brightly, letting off a large cloud of smoke.

items.title: UAE slams 'dangerous escalation' after drone hits near nuclear plant

children.content: Abu Dhabi officials say radiation levels are normal and there was no damage to the plant, but authorities are now working to determine who carried out the attack.

image.altText: The exterior of a nuclear power station in the desert.

items.title: Advocates say more women being coerced and threatened to leave Australia

children.content: Advocates for migrant women say they're seeing more cases of exit trafficking — where an offender uses coercion, threats or deception to organise a person's departure from Australia.

gallery.altText: A clip art image of a woman, passport and cancelled stamp

thumbnail.altText: A clip art image of a woman, passport and cancelled stamp

items.title: WHO declares Ebola outbreak an 'emergency of international concern'

children.content: At least 80 people have died in what the World Health Organization says potentially could be a "much larger outbreak than what is currently being detected and reported".

image.altText: A man takes the temperature of another man using a thermometer. He's wearing a mask.

items.title: Criminal cases at risk of being halted as NT legal aid crisis worsens

children.content: Lawyers in the Northern Territory are commencing a landmark legal challenge that could require courts to halt some cases due to defendants being unable to access a lawyer amid a worsening legal aid crisis.

image.altText: A man with a serious expression sits on a long wooden table with legal books behind him.

items.title: The dilemma Wallabies and All Blacks face when players head overseas

children.content: Both Australia and New Zealand strive to protect Super Rugby Pacific as their premier domestic competition, even it if it has consequences for the Wallabies and All Blacks.

gallery.altText: Len Ikitau tackled by a Bath opponent whiole playing for Exeter.

thumbnail.altText: Len Ikitau tackled by a Bath opponent whiole playing for Exeter.

items.title: Late bogey costs Cameron Smith after PGA Championship surge

children.content: Australian Cameron Smith makes a hot start to the final round at the PGA Championship, putting himself right in the mix to end his major drought, before tripping up on the penultimate hole.

image.altText: Cameron Smith gestures with his hand after putting at the PGA Championship.

items.title: More rain forecast for south-east Queensland before temperatures plummet

children.content: Coastal rain will linger today and tomorrow, before clear skies and and chilly nights arrive on Wednesday.

image.altText: beach gloomy weather

items.title: Steel and lead are facing trial by fire in SA's regional cities

children.content: The South Australian government has put all its chips on the table for the Whyalla steelworks and Port Pirie lead smelter. The next six months will be critical.

image.altText: A worker at the face of the blast furnace at the Whyalla steelworks.

items.title: New foreshore plan for historic Sydney park revealed

children.content: A new foreshore plan for Callan Park in Sydney's inner west includes a cafe, but the future of commercial opportunities at the site depends now on the passing of amended legislation.

image.altText: A wide aerial shot of a green parkland near the water, called Callan Park.

items.title: Elk have been spotted in the wild in SA. Where did they come from?

children.content: A South Australian landowner has spotted elk on her property, outside containment lines of deer farms. There are no known established wild elk herds in this state.

gallery.altText: two large elk stand next to bushland.

thumbnail.altText: two large elk stand next to bushland.

items.title: Kittens turned away from shelters amid 'heartbreaking' stray cat crisis

children.content: Animal shelters across New South Wales sound the alarm on a worsening stray cat crisis.

image.altText: 4 kittens huddling together on a towel

items.title: Students of newly merged Adelaide University protest changing of graduation venue

children.content: The newly merged Adelaide University has changed the venue of its graduation ceremonies from Bonython Hall, to Pridham Hall with students launching a petition in opposition.

image.altText: Bonython Hall - an old heritage brick building in the Adelaide CBD

items.title: 'Devoted father and gentleman': Tributes flow for shark attack victim

children.content: The wife of a 38-year-old Perth man who died in a shark attack off the coast of Rottnest Island in Western Australia on Saturday describes him as a devoted father and avid spearfisherman.
